Frequently Asked Questions About WICS Committees
2024 – 2025 Committee
General Questions
What are coffee chats like and what advice do you have for them?
They are 15-minute casual “interviews”. Some questions include what your hobbies, why are you interested in WICS Committee and other questions based on your application. Coffee chats help us understand who you are. Chairs of the committee that you’ve been selected to interview for will chat with you. Show your personality and genuine enthusiasm, let them see that you’re excited and want to be involved. Doing a bit of prep, like reflecting on your experiences or doing some quick research on the committee to see what events or initiatives resonate with you, could help you feel more confident since that gives you things to talk about and helps you figure out if it’s a good fit. Overall, coffee chats are friendly and low-pressure, just be yourself and show your excitement to be part of WICS!
How many people are you looking for in each committee?
We are not announcing the numbers, instead, we urge you to apply to the committee that you feel most passionate about! Don’t apply to a committee because it has more spots available! Since we have preferences, if you have a strong application/interview you will be able to join a committee outside of your first choice.
How many people are in charge of each committee?
Each committee has two committee chairs who oversee its projects, events, and members, helping everything run smoothly and ensuring everyone feels supported. In addition, we have our two wonderful presidents who guide WICS as a whole, providing leadership, encouragement, and direction for all committees.
Are we able to apply to multiple committees?
Yes. On our application form, we have first preference, second preference, and third preference.
Your deliverable will just be for the first preference.
No need to make deliverables for second/third preference.
When spring rolls around, you have the opportunity to switch committees for the next school year, so you have the chance to experience other committees.
Is it okay to miss committee meetings and general meetings if you have conflicts?
We understand that everyone has their schedules, and there will be accommodations if you can’t attend the meetings. In general, you would let the secretary know, and we will make sure to get notes and support. At the beginning of every quarter, we will schedule our committee meetings at times that work best for most people’s calendars.
How have you seen WICS change over the years?
WICS has been around for over 30 years now, and there’s so much amazing documentation and history behind it. Every new group of members wants to build on that and make things even better. We rotate presidents every year and get to see what changes each one brings. For example, the types of socials or how they run things can be different, and that’s something we embrace. We also take feedback from the committee seriously and try to improve each year.
What do you wish you knew before being on WICS committee?
Being on WICS committee is full of opportunities to grow both personally and professionally, so take advantage of every opportunity that comes your way and don’t be afraid to step outside your comfort zone! The effort you put into WICS truly reflects in what you gain, from expanding your network to gaining skills and confidence. But that means there’s always a lot happening behind the scenes, so don’t hesitate to ask for help when you need it, everyone is here to support you!
Committee Questions
Internal Affairs
External Affairs
Marketing
Community Outreach
Answered by Josephine Bhadran & Kristen Yee
What was your favorite event that you either planned or attended?
Josephine: My favorite event was WICSino. I helped plan it with a few other members, and we set up different booths with games. I actually learned how to deal blackjack, which was really fun, and overall I just had a great time.
Kristen: Event collab with Video game design club where you got to bring ICS perspectives into mad libs and try something new. Was able to participate in it and plan it as well. Had a great time.
What drew you to the specific committee you are on when you were applying?
Josephine: I was deciding between a few committees at first, but I’ve always liked logistics and event planning. When I thought about what I enjoy most, which is planning fun, social events where everyone can just hang out, I realized Internal was the best fit!
Kristen: Wants to give back to the mentorship program, really enjoys planning all the events, and loves the community aspect.
What is your favorite part of being on WICS committee? What do you value most?
Josephine: The community! During my first year of college, I was really scared and intimidated, so going into my second year I told myself I had to get involved. I’ve met so many amazing people, and I love spending time with everyone, whether it’s chatting or working together on events. I’ve gained so much from this community and really enjoy being able to give back now.
Kristen: After joining WICS, Kristen found a community in many of her classes. She was able to go into class and realize that there were girls she already knew. She met most of her friends through the supportive and kind community here.
What would you say is the most important skill you would have to develop in each of the committees
Kristen: Public speaking and planning/organization are skills you really develop in Internal. You learn to develop adaptability and grow yourself in your committee. Even if you don’t come on to the committee with the skill, you will get many chances to practice and lots of support.
Describe the process of planning an event.
Josephine: For Internal, sometimes the co-chairs will assign us an event, and you’ll work with a few other members to plan and complete it. Other times, it’s more open-ended and we brainstorm ideas together. It’s a lot of communicating with your group, making slides, ordering materials, and organizing everything for the event.
What are examples of the ways different committees collaborate with each other?
Kristen: Internal interacts with Marketing and External, as well as getting help from other committees. There are always ways to help on other committees with various tasks, and we’re open to requesting help from others
Answered by Emily Tom & Kara Statham
What was your favorite event that you either planned or attended?
Emily: My favorite event was Mock Technical Interviews. It felt really personalized, and I got to take notes and see what I could improve on for the future!
Kara: My favorite event was the mentorship mixer. Going to a mixer was an easy way to get to know people. Great just talking to people, seeing new and old faces. Mock technical interviews are also super fun to plan, involves reaching out to a lot of company reps and getting a lot of people involved. Full committee thing and everyone is able to help. Very big learning experience figuring out how to send professional emails and being this link between a corporate liaison and WICS.
What drew you to the specific committee you are on when you were applying?
Emily: I did a lot of event planning in high school and was choosing between Internal and External, but I wanted to grow more professionally, so I went with External.
Kara: Thought it was a good opportunity to get to learn networking skills through external.
What is your favorite part of being on WICS committee? What do you value most?
Emily: For me, it’s definitely the network and the community. You get to build closer relationships with the people on your committee since you see them so often, and you end up making so many friends.
Kara: You are engulfed into a family and someone is always in one of your classes. You always have someone to vent to about homework and professors too.
Describe the process of planning an event.
Emily: For External, at the start of the quarter, you’re assigned an event and the week it’ll happen. It’s usually pre-set which company or club you’ll work with, but you can give your preferences. You typically work with one or two other committee members to reach out to that company about availability. Sometimes they already have a plan for the event, and we’ll meet a couple times to review it, make suggestions, and finalize everything. Usually about two or three meetings total.
What are examples of the ways different committees collaborate with each other?
Kara: Since external affairs is in charge of planning half of general events, they try to shuffle it as best as possible to split up the workload with internal affairs. They also work with marketing such as sending the blurb, connecting with company reps, etc.
What would you say is the most important skill you would have to develop in each of the committees?
Kara: Not exactly a skill but a willingness to try something new. Being able to put yourself out there and external will always be there to support you.
Answered by Jyoti Maharjan, Shalini Bhakta, & Sophia Shaw
What was your favorite event that you either planned or attended?
Jyoti: I’d say End-of-year Banquets. It’s super sweet getting to see everyone I’d met throughout the year all in one place.
Shalini: With marketing, they don’t really get to do collaboration events. One of the favorite things they started last year was starting to do TikToks and collaborating with other clubs on social media. It’s a chance to be creative and connect with other clubs. Crepe boothing is also a fun bonding experience!
What drew you to the specific committee you are on when you were applying?
Jyoti: I joined Marketing because I did yearbook and marketing for clubs back in high school, so I felt that is where I can contribute to the most. The community here has been amazing and honestly what made me stay all four years.
Shalini: WICS put a lot of effort into making a place for themselves on social media platforms. From Facebook to Instagram to Tiktok, WICS is always actively growing and staying true to it’s cute aesthetic.
What is your favorite part of being on WICS committee? What do you value most?
Jyoti: Some of my best friends, even going into my fourth year, came from WICS. This community is really special to me!
Shalini: Always has a community that supports you in classes. Really love the community and throughout committees there’s a lot of opportunities to get to know other girls with different passions and majors. Everyone is super nice.
Describe the process of planning Marketing tasks.
Sophia: For Marketing, we handle things like TikToks, LinkedIn, and Instagram. There’s also a graphic design team, and we collaborate with all the other committees to create content. We schedule posts, spend a lot of time on socials doing research or posting updates, and we’re also the ones who design the merch, like t-shirts and tote bags. We even do club collabs to film TikToks, which is always really fun!
What are examples of the ways different committees collaborate with each other?
Shalini: Lots of collaboration with all the other committees. Marketing is the bridge of communication from all the committees to our audience. We reach out to all the committee members to create social media content. Furthermore, marketing has amazing feedback on the general events, since we can judge the planning from an outside perspective.
What would you say is the most important skill you would have to develop in each of the committees?
Shalini: Creativity! Even if you don’t come with creative ideas, just be open to new things. Be ready to be adaptive and have the opportunity to transform WICS’s presence on campus. Everything is collaborative and ideas are put into play.
Answered by Viviana Salazar & Sarah Tan
What was your favorite event that you either planned or attended?
Viviana: My favorite outreach event was NetWICS. I especially liked the first day. Each UCI club had their own station with a technical project, and I thought that was so cool. I also really enjoyed the smaller moments, like chatting with students during lunch. It was great seeing how excited they were about their future careers!
Sarah: WICS x Game Dev Club, a relaxed and creative event where everyone got to talk and bond over their game ideas. First Outreach event teaching HTML to younger students. Even though I was worried that my knowledge wasn’t enough, I got to support students in creating amazing projects. No one called me out, everyone supported me in turn as we taught the students. Also bonded with them over common interests (TXT)!
What drew you to the specific committee you are on when you were applying?
Viviana: I chose Outreach since that’s what I had the most experience with! In high school, I used to mentor middle school robotics teams. I wasn’t really set on one committee at first, so I always tell people it’s fine to have a few in mind. I ended up picking Outreach because it’s where I could put the most energy and passion, and then later transitioned to External when I ran for Treasurer and got the position.
Sarah: Was a stem counselor at a camp previously and wanted to continue supporting younger students in their CS interests. Outreach gives you the opportunity to help younger girls learn the incredible things that computers can do. Especially for those without the resources to learn about computer science and tech, being able to spark their passion is so rewarding.
What is your favorite part of being on WICS committee? What do you value most?
Viviana: I didn’t know anyone else in college or in computer science, so WICS became a space where I could talk to people, ask questions, and learn about the resources UCI has to offer. I’ve also really liked practicing and developing soft skills, which are things you don’t always think about, and growing my leadership skills through it.
Sarah: Found opportunities to bond with other female students in more personal ways than just class. Met others through the retreat and has developed close friendships.
Describe the process of planning an event.
Viviana: In Outreach, the co-chairs usually come up with event ideas and then delegate different tasks to members. There’s a lot of variety! I’ve helped make games during breaks, created slide decks and event content, and designed posters or graphics. You don’t always do the same task every time, which keeps it fun.
What are examples of the ways different committees collaborate with each other?
Sarah: Outreach is not as involved with general meetings. They collaborate with marketing a lot since they need helpers for events (people with experience teaching, etc), and getting word out about the events. They also collaborate with the graphic designers.
What would you say is the most important skill you would have to develop in each of the committees?
Sarah: Communication whether you are working with logistics or teaching the content, you want to be able to communicate with the kids in a way they want to listen and want to learn. Be able to provide a positive environment for the girls so they can have a new experience and have something to look back on.

WICS Committee Applications are closed!
Applications will reopen at the beginning of each academic year.
Click here for
FAQS!
Being on the WICS Committee is a great way to get involved, meet people, and grow your leadership skills. Committee members gain valuable experience in
networking, event planning, and project management
, and work closely with company representatives and other ICS clubs throughout the year. It’s also an amazing resume booster that shows initiative, teamwork, and leadership to future employers and grad programs.
On average, committee members spend about
3+ hours
a week
on meetings and tasks, though time can pick up a bit before big events. Committee members are expected to take part in WICS events throughout the year to stay engaged and contribute to the community, with the exception of classes (don’t worry, classes always come first!).
Some of our main initiatives and events include:
Mock Technical Interviews
in the Winter quarter that involves having mock technical interviews with real company engineers.
WICS Games
where students and company representatives come together for a night of friendly competition, learning, and prizes!
Weekly General Meetings
that are a mix of social and professional events for members to learn, connect, and have fun!
Year-long Mentorship Program
where we pair experienced upperclassmen with mentees to build community and grow together (and make a competition out of it too!).
Through
Community Outreach
, we inspire the next generation by teaching computer science and engineering concepts in local schools using fun tools like littleBits and app jams.
2024-25 WICS Committee
External Affairs
Host professional development events with companies
Tech talks & panels
Networking nights
Info sessions & workshops
Bring in company sponsorships to support WICS events
Connect students with industry representatives
Internal Affairs
Host social/community development events
General events & club collabs
Game Nights, Arts & Crafts, Mixers
Retreats & Banquet
Study sessions/workshops
Mentorship socials
Marketing
Maintain the public presence of WICS on social media, newsletters, booths, etc.
Promote WICS and its opportunities to the greater UCI community
Build community and promote diversity in the field
Community Outreach
Host workshops and conferences for local K-12 schools
Our goals include:
Teach students about coding fundamentals and beginner languages (i.e. Scratch)
Hold coffee chats and networking opportunities between K-12 students and college mentors
Plan fun activities to build connections between students
Grow their aspirations in CS

Each committee has two committee chairs who oversee its projects, events, and members, helping everything run smoothly and ensuring everyone feels supported. In addition, we have our two wonderful presidents who guide WICS as a whole, providing leadership, encouragement, and direction for all committees.
Are we able to apply to multiple committees?
Yes. On our application form, we have first preference, second preference, and third preference.
Your deliverable will just be for the first preference.
No need to make deliverables for second/third preference.
When spring rolls around, you have the opportunity to switch committees for the next school year, so you have the chance to experience other committees.
Is it okay to miss committee meetings and general meetings if you have conflicts?
We understand that everyone has their schedules, and there will be accommodations if you can’t attend the meetings. In general, you would let the secretary know, and we will make sure to get notes and support. At the beginning of every quarter, we will schedule our committee meetings at times that work best for most people’s calendars.
How have you seen WICS change over the years?
WICS has been around for over 30 years now, and there’s so much amazing documentation and history behind it. Every new group of members wants to build on that and make things even better. We rotate presidents every year and get to see what changes each one brings. For example, the types of socials or how they run things can be different, and that’s something we embrace. We also take feedback from the committee seriously and try to improve each year.
What do you wish you knew before being on WICS committee?
Being on WICS committee is full of opportunities to grow both personally and professionally, so take advantage of every opportunity that comes your way and don’t be afraid to step outside your comfort zone! The effort you put into WICS truly reflects in what you gain, from expanding your network to gaining skills and confidence. But that means there’s always a lot happening behind the scenes, so don’t hesitate to ask for help when you need it, everyone is here to support you!
Committee Questions
Internal Affairs
External Affairs
Marketing
Community Outreach
Answered by Josephine Bhadran & Kristen Yee
What was your favorite event that you either planned or attended?
Josephine: My favorite event was WICSino. I helped plan it with a few other members, and we set up different booths with games. I actually learned how to deal blackjack, which was really fun, and overall I just had a great time.
Kristen: Event collab with Video game design club where you got to bring ICS perspectives into mad libs and try something new. Was able to participate in it and plan it as well. Had a great time.
What drew you to the specific committee you are on when you were applying?
Josephine: I was deciding between a few committees at first, but I’ve always liked logistics and event planning. When I thought about what I enjoy most, which is planning fun, social events where everyone can just hang out, I realized Internal was the best fit!
Kristen: Wants to give back to the mentorship program, really enjoys planning all the events, and loves the community aspect.
What is your favorite part of being on WICS committee? What do you value most?
Josephine: The community! During my first year of college, I was really scared and intimidated, so going into my second year I told myself I had to get involved. I’ve met so many amazing people, and I love spending time with everyone, whether it’s chatting or working together on events. I’ve gained so much from this community and really enjoy being able to give back now.
Kristen: After joining WICS, Kristen found a community in many of her classes. She was able to go into class and realize that there were girls she already knew. She met most of her friends through the supportive and kind community here.
What would you say is the most important skill you would have to develop in each of the committees
Kristen: Public speaking and planning/organization are skills you really develop in Internal. You learn to develop adaptability and grow yourself in your committee. Even if you don’t come on to the committee with the skill, you will get many chances to practice and lots of support.
Describe the process of planning an event.
Josephine: For Internal, sometimes the co-chairs will assign us an event, and you’ll work with a few other members to plan and complete it. Other times, it’s more open-ended and we brainstorm ideas together. It’s a lot of communicating with your group, making slides, ordering materials, and organizing everything for the event.
What are examples of the ways different committees collaborate with each other?
Kristen: Internal interacts with Marketing and External, as well as getting help from other committees. There are always ways to help on other committees with various tasks, and we’re open to requesting help from others
Answered by Emily Tom & Kara Statham
What was your favorite event that you either planned or attended?
Emily: My favorite event was Mock Technical Interviews. It felt really personalized, and I got to take notes and see what I could improve on for the future!
Kara: My favorite event was the mentorship mixer. Going to a mixer was an easy way to get to know people. Great just talking to people, seeing new and old faces. Mock technical interviews are also super fun to plan, involves reaching out to a lot of company reps and getting a lot of people involved. Full committee thing and everyone is able to help. Very big learning experience figuring out how to send professional emails and being this link between a corporate liaison and WICS.
What drew you to the specific committee you are on when you were applying?
Emily: I did a lot of event planning in high school and was choosing between Internal and External, but I wanted to grow more professionally, so I went with External.
Kara: Thought it was a good opportunity to get to learn networking skills through external.
What is your favorite part of being on WICS committee? What do you value most?
Emily: For me, it’s definitely the network and the community. You get to build closer relationships with the people on your committee since you see them so often, and you end up making so many friends.
Kara: You are engulfed into a family and someone is always in one of your classes. You always have someone to vent to about homework and professors too.
Describe the process of planning an event.
Emily: For External, at the start of the quarter, you’re assigned an event and the week it’ll happen. It’s usually pre-set which company or club you’ll work with, but you can give your preferences. You typically work with one or two other committee members to reach out to that company about availability. Sometimes they already have a plan for the event, and we’ll meet a couple times to review it, make suggestions, and finalize everything. Usually about two or three meetings total.
What are examples of the ways different committees collaborate with each other?
Kara: Since external affairs is in charge of planning half of general events, they try to shuffle it as best as possible to split up the workload with internal affairs. They also work with marketing such as sending the blurb, connecting with company reps, etc.
What would you say is the most important skill you would have to develop in each of the committees?
Kara: Not exactly a skill but a willingness to try something new. Being able to put yourself out there and external will always be there to support you.
Answered by Jyoti Maharjan, Shalini Bhakta, & Sophia Shaw
What was your favorite event that you either planned or attended?
Jyoti: I’d say End-of-year Banquets. It’s super sweet getting to see everyone I’d met throughout the year all in one place.
Shalini: With marketing, they don’t really get to do collaboration events. One of the favorite things they started last year was starting to do TikToks and collaborating with other clubs on social media. It’s a chance to be creative and connect with other clubs. Crepe boothing is also a fun bonding experience!
What drew you to the specific committee you are on when you were applying?
Jyoti: I joined Marketing because I did yearbook and marketing for clubs back in high school, so I felt that is where I can contribute to the most. The community here has been amazing and honestly what made me stay all four years.
Shalini: WICS put a lot of effort into making a place for themselves on social media platforms. From Facebook to Instagram to Tiktok, WICS is always actively growing and staying true to it’s cute aesthetic.
What is your favorite part of being on WICS committee? What do you value most?
Jyoti: Some of my best friends, even going into my fourth year, came from WICS. This community is really special to me!
Shalini: Always has a community that supports you in classes. Really love the community and throughout committees there’s a lot of opportunities to get to know other girls with different passions and majors. Everyone is super nice.
Describe the process of planning Marketing tasks.
Sophia: For Marketing, we handle things like TikToks, LinkedIn, and Instagram. There’s also a graphic design team, and we collaborate with all the other committees to create content. We schedule posts, spend a lot of time on socials doing research or posting updates, and we’re also the ones who design the merch, like t-shirts and tote bags. We even do club collabs to film TikToks, which is always really fun!
What are examples of the ways different committees collaborate with each other?
Shalini: Lots of collaboration with all the other committees. Marketing is the bridge of communication from all the committees to our audience. We reach out to all the committee members to create social media content. Furthermore, marketing has amazing feedback on the general events, since we can judge the planning from an outside perspective.
What would you say is the most important skill you would have to develop in each of the committees?
Shalini: Creativity! Even if you don’t come with creative ideas, just be open to new things. Be ready to be adaptive and have the opportunity to transform WICS’s presence on campus. Everything is collaborative and ideas are put into play.
Answered by Viviana Salazar & Sarah Tan
What was your favorite event that you either planned or attended?
Viviana: My favorite outreach event was NetWICS. I especially liked the first day. Each UCI club had their own station with a technical project, and I thought that was so cool. I also really enjoyed the smaller moments, like chatting with students during lunch. It was great seeing how excited they were about their future careers!
Sarah: WICS x Game Dev Club, a relaxed and creative event where everyone got to talk and bond over their game ideas. First Outreach event teaching HTML to younger students. Even though I was worried that my knowledge wasn’t enough, I got to support students in creating amazing projects. No one called me out, everyone supported me in turn as we taught the students. Also bonded with them over common interests (TXT)!
What drew you to the specific committee you are on when you were applying?
Viviana: I chose Outreach since that’s what I had the most experience with! In high school, I used to mentor middle school robotics teams. I wasn’t really set on one committee at first, so I always tell people it’s fine to have a few in mind. I ended up picking Outreach because it’s where I could put the most energy and passion, and then later transitioned to External when I ran for Treasurer and got the position.
Sarah: Was a stem counselor at a camp previously and wanted to continue supporting younger students in their CS interests. Outreach gives you the opportunity to help younger girls learn the incredible things that computers can do. Especially for those without the resources to learn about computer science and tech, being able to spark their passion is so rewarding.
What is your favorite part of being on WICS committee? What do you value most?
Viviana: I didn’t know anyone else in college or in computer science, so WICS became a space where I could talk to people, ask questions, and learn about the resources UCI has to offer. I’ve also really liked practicing and developing soft skills, which are things you don’t always think about, and growing my leadership skills through it.
Sarah: Found opportunities to bond with other female students in more personal ways than just class. Met others through the retreat and has developed close friendships.
Describe the process of planning an event.
Viviana: In Outreach, the co-chairs usually come up with event ideas and then delegate different tasks to members. There’s a lot of variety! I’ve helped make games during breaks, created slide decks and event content, and designed posters or graphics. You don’t always do the same task every time, which keeps it fun.
What are examples of the ways different committees collaborate with each other?
Sarah: Outreach is not as involved with general meetings. They collaborate with marketing a lot since they need helpers for events (people with experience teaching, etc), and getting word out about the events. They also collaborate with the graphic designers.
What would you say is the most important skill you would have to develop in each of the committees?
Sarah: Communication whether you are working with logistics or teaching the content, you want to be able to communicate with the kids in a way they want to listen and want to learn. Be able to provide a positive environment for the girls so they can have a new experience and have something to look back on.

Being on the WICS Committee is a great way to get involved, meet people, and grow your leadership skills. Committee members gain valuable experience in
networking, event planning, and project management
, and work closely with company representatives and other ICS clubs throughout the year. It’s also an amazing resume booster that shows initiative, teamwork, and leadership to future employers and grad programs.
On average, committee members spend about
3+ hours
a week
on meetings and tasks, though time can pick up a bit before big events. Committee members are expected to take part in WICS events throughout the year to stay engaged and contribute to the community, with the exception of classes (don’t worry, classes always come first!).
Some of our main initiatives and events include:
Mock Technical Interviews
in the Winter quarter that involves having mock technical interviews with real company engineers.
WICS Games
where students and company representatives come together for a night of friendly competition, learning, and prizes!
Weekly General Meetings
that are a mix of social and professional events for members to learn, connect, and have fun!
Year-long Mentorship Program
where we pair experienced upperclassmen with mentees to build community and grow together (and make a competition out of it too!).
Through
Community Outreach
, we inspire the next generation by teaching computer science and engineering concepts in local schools using fun tools like littleBits and app jams.
2024-25 WICS Committee
External Affairs
Host professional development events with companies
Tech talks & panels
Networking nights
Info sessions & workshops
Bring in company sponsorships to support WICS events
Connect students with industry representatives
Internal Affairs
Host social/community development events
General events & club collabs
Game Nights, Arts & Crafts, Mixers
Retreats & Banquet
Study sessions/workshops
Mentorship socials
Marketing
Maintain the public presence of WICS on social media, newsletters, booths, etc.
Promote WICS and its opportunities to the greater UCI community
Build community and promote diversity in the field
Community Outreach
Host workshops and conferences for local K-12 schools
Our goals include:
Teach students about coding fundamentals and beginner languages (i.e. Scratch)
Hold coffee chats and networking opportunities between K-12 students and college mentors
Plan fun activities to build connections between students
Grow their aspirations in CS

WICS Mentorship Program
The 2025-26 WICS Mentorship program is now closed! The program will reopen at the start of the next academic year, but in the meantime, check out some of out past mentorship events below and in our
blog posts
!
Every year WICS hosts a mentorship program. We pair up upperclassmen mentors with underclassmen in ICS. Our mentors provide not just tutoring, but moral support and general guidance. Many of our mentors and mentees have become good friends and stay in touch long after our program ends! Mentees benefit significantly for having experienced and relatable guidance in their first years, and our mentors can stand out to companies by working with our organization. This year, our mentorship program is using the family system, where each family consists of 2-3 mentors and who each mentor 2 mentees. This way, mentors will be accompanied by their co-mentors during the program, and mentees are able to bond with the others in the family as well.
“I joined the program to get technical advice on networking, passing interviews, and landing internships. Kristen Yee was the best mentor I could’ve ever asked for – she helped me with mock technical interviews, offered her experience with talking to professionals, and best of all, took me out to eat! I was able to grow my confidence and land my first summer internship with her help, and I made a lifelong friend :3 I recommend this program because it provides mentees with a support system to navigate school, life, and their career, and it gives mentors a chance to give back to the community!”
– Kristen Chen,
Mentee
“I decided to join the mentorship program as a first year to learn more about CS at UCI and gain advice from upperclassmen. After experiencing how helpful the program was, I became a mentor the following year so I could pass on the guidance and opportunities my mentors had shared with me. The mentorship program is an incredible way to learn from each other and then pay that knowledge forward. I wouldn’t be where I am in my tech journey today without the support of both my mentors and mentees.”
– Rhea Shetti,
Mentor
“I decided to join a mentorship program to find a group of CS students who could help me navigate my college experience. Before WICS mentorship, I didn’t know anyone else in the CS field. Through WICS mentorship, I was able to get my questions answered, discover helpful resources, and gain much more confidence in myself as a CS student because I always had someone to reach out to. Most importantly, it introduced me to an amazing group of friends at UCI who I love seeing every week at WICS!”
– Viviana Salazar,
Mentee
“Coming into WICS without knowing anyone was intimidating, but the mentorship program provided me the opportunity to get to know so many people, especially my wonderful mentor, Celine! She served as a great role model for her mentorship family, embodying everything I strive to be—passionate, driven, involved, and personable. Throughout the program, Celine reached out to me with support and encouragement to pursue whatever I set my mind on, including applying for WICS committee and running for Internal Affairs Vice President! I’m so thankful for the program as well as the community and opportunities that have come with it!”
– Megan Sun,
Mentee

Join Us!
WICS meetings are open and free to all students at the University of California, Irvine, regardless of background (major, gender, year, etc). Any student who attends a WICS meeting will automatically be considered a
general member
.
General Membership Benefits:
All the members have the right to be informed about all planned WICS events
All members have the right to benefit from our events such as networking opportunities with company representatives
All members have the right to make constructive suggestions or discuss any issues that may arise with the club or any board or committee member
If you want to be more involved with WICS check out our
Mentorship Program
page or
Apply to be a WICS Committee Member
page.
Sign up for our mailing list below or
on our signup form

IrisHacks 2023
On May 6th and 7th, WICS’ Community Outreach hosted, in collaboration with PCWOC, a 2-day hackathon for beginner hackers. 13 UCI, community college, and high school students came to the UCI campus to work together to build a project from scratch. The participants attended a short workshop series introducing fundamental tools, such as HTML/CSS, Git, SQL, and APIs, that they could use in their projects. After 24 hours of hacking, the students presented their project to a panel of judges to win some prizes. Congrats to all the winners!

Spring 2023 Week 6: WICS Resume Workshop
On May 8th, 2023, WICS hosted a resume workshop with Northrop Grumman. Lexie Huffman, a university recruiter from Northrop, gave a detailed presentation over Zoom about creating a stand-out resume. WICS members gained lots of valuable tips and insight into the different components that make up a good resume. For the second half of the workshop, attendees worked in teams to find and correct errors on a sample resume for none other than “Peter Anteater”.

Spring 2023 Week 7: WICS x CTC Git Workshop
WICS had a special visit from Commit the Change (CTC) on May 15th, 2023. We recently hosted an engaging and informative event—a Git workshop that left participants with valuable knowledge and hands-on experience. The workshop featured multiple demos covering essential topics such as cloning repositories, pushing changes to remote repositories, creating branches, and resolving Git conflicts. The attendees enthusiastically followed along, actively participating in the workshop. To conclude the event on a fun note, a small playground repository was introduced. This gave everyone the opportunity to practice their newfound skills by pushing their favorite memes to a website hosted within that repository. The event fostered a sense of collaboration and learning, leaving participants empowered with practical Git expertise and a memorable experience.

Spring 2023 Week 9: WICS General Retreat
On Saturday of Week 9, WICS hosted General Retreat! Members headed to Corona del Mar for an afternoon of fun in the sun. Equipped with sunscreen and hats, members enjoyed snacks while they chatted on beach towels. They indulged in a variety of activities, including dipping their feet in the cold ocean water, climbing rocks, observing tide pool wildlife, building sandcastles, digging holes, and making s’mores around a cozy bonfire. Overall, this was a super fun day and a great opportunity for members to meet other members outside of school.

Spring 2023 Week 10: WICS Banquet
On Monday, June 5, 2023, WICS celebrated the last event of the school year with a banquet. WICS members gathered together to eat and socialize with each other. The event sponsor, Notion@UCI, held a Notion Workspace walkthrough to show students how they can organize their code and notes for classes. WICS then revealed mentorship prizes for the winning teams and silly awards for everyone to appreciate their superlatives. WICS also congratulated graduating seniors and had a photo wall for members to take pictures in front of.

The workshop featured multiple demos covering essential topics such as cloning repositories, pushing changes to remote repositories, creating branches, and resolving Git conflicts. The attendees enthusiastically followed along, actively participating in the workshop. To conclude the event on a fun note, a small playground repository was introduced. This gave everyone the opportunity to practice their newfound skills by pushing their favorite memes to a website hosted within that repository. The event fostered a sense of collaboration and learning, leaving participants empowered with practical Git expertise and a memorable experience.

This event gave members the opportunity to connect with students who have previously interned at a variety of companies. Participants rotated through small groups, where they asked questions to the panelists about the steps they took to land their internships, what their day-to-day responsibilities looked like, and tips for navigating the internship process. It was a valuable and inspiring evening filled with insight, advice, and meaningful conversations!

Spring 2025: Anteater Awards
We’re beyond excited and grateful to share that WICS has been awarded the 2025 Most Outstanding Organization of the Year at the Anteater Awards!
This recognition is a celebration of everyone who makes WICS a supportive and ever-growing community filled with people who are passionate about technology.
To our 11 other board members: thank you for your nonstop dedication since last summer. From socials and study sessions to outreach and professional development, your heart is in every event we host. Your leadership and genuine efforts to provide opportunities for ownership and collaboration is truly what makes our committee better and better every year.
To our 38 committee members: thank you for your commitment, altruism and passion for supporting young techmakers. Your weekly efforts in curating meaningful events is what brings WICS to greater heights.
To our 600+ general members: thank you for showing up. Whether it’s for mentorship, workshops, or just good vibes at game night, you are the soul of this community. Even if it was just one event, we hope that you were able to take away something valuable and hope to see you again in the fall!
To our fellow ICS clubs and company sponsors: we are so grateful for your partnership and collaboration. Building a stronger tech community is always better together. And to our amazing advisors, Dr. Debra Richardson, Dr. Jennifer Wong-Ma, and Vinh Luong thank you for your advice and guidance throughout the years. We couldn’t be where we are without you!
This award means a lot, but the community we’ve built together means even more. Thank you all for being part of WICS.
With love and appreciation,
Aasha Sendhil & Sanika Bhamare
2024–2025 Co-Presidents

Fall 2025 Week 1: First General Meeting
On Monday, September 29, 2025, WICS kicked off the year with our First General Meeting in DBH 6011, bringing together over 150 students for an evening of community and connection. Attendees got to dive into committee-led activities like Speed NetWICSing, the WICS Mix, #MarketingMoment, and Elementary Explanations, each offering a fun way to meet members and learn more about what WICS has to offer. With a lively photobooth, free pizza, and raffle prizes to close out the night, new and returning members alike had the chance to get involved, make new friends, and celebrate the start of another exciting year with WICS!

Fall 2025 Week 2: Mentorship Mixer
This Monday, October 6, 2025, WICS welcomed it’s members to the first mentorship event of the year. The meeting began with announcing this year’s mentorship family theme: “fruits” which symbolizes the fruitful friendships and community that mentorship will bring this year. Following this, we began our rotations, where prospective mentees spent five minutes with small groups of mentors answering two questions about themselves. These questions helped attendees get to know each other and will hopefully guide them in selecting which mentors they’d like to be paired with. Overall, the event was lively and filled with so much chatter and excitement for another year of mentorship!

Fall 2025 Week 5: Conferences Workshop with the Dev Difference
On Monday, October 27, 2025, WICS had the pleasure of collaborating with The Dev Difference to host a conference workshop. In this workshop, members were guided by one of the company’s co-founders, Jovanay Carter, in planning out their goals for future conferences and career fairs, and what steps they will take to achieve those goals. Goals ranged from getting an interview, expanding networks, or even securing an internship. In addition, they brainstormed outlines for elevator pitches and shared them with other students for feedback. Several members got to present their pitches to the whole group in the final activity.

Fall 2025 Week 6: Mix & Mingle
On Monday, November 3, 2025, WICS teamed up with Commit the Change (CTC) to organize a networking mixer in the style of Squid Games’ Mingle activity for their club members. Students had the opportunity to practice their networking skills, form new connections, and share and learn insights about the various fields in the technology industry. As the game went on groups became larger and the length of mingling time also increased, which gave students the chance to participate in a variety of meaningful conversations. At the end of the event, students gained access to a networking sheet where they could add their contact information (LinkedIn, social media, etc.)!

Spring 2025 Week 1: Among WICS
On Monday, March 31 2025, WICS kicked off Spring quarter with their First General Meeting, Among WICS! To start, WICS gave an introduction to the organization, detailing insight to its committees, information of upcoming events, and how to keep up with WICS. Then, members found the impostors among them by forming groups and playing rounds of the online game Among Us! Those who won the most points got free WICS T-shirts as their reward. Overall, whether they were a crewmate or an impostor, attendees all had a fun, exhilarating time at WICS’ first meeting of the quarter, and got to enjoy gaming with fellow members!

Spring 2025 Week 2: WICS x WiT Coffee Chat
On Monday, April 7, 2025, WICS collaborated with Women in Tech (WiT) at UCI to host an engaging coffee chat. Members gathered in seven circular groups, each with a representative from WiT. The event was structured into five 14-minute rounds with quick one-minute rotations between sessions. During each round, participants had the opportunity to ask WiT representatives about their personal journeys and experiences in various tech subfields. The intimate setting allowed for meaningful conversations and insights, with members eagerly jotting down advice and exchanging contact information. Some groups erupted in laughter over shared experiences, while others engaged in deep discussions about navigating the tech industry.

Spring 2025 Week 3: CoStar Office Tour
On Thursday, April 17, 2025, during Week 3 of the spring quarter, WICS had the exciting opportunity to tour CoStar Group’s office in Irvine! Members were warmly welcomed and treated to a delicious lunch to kick off the visit. After lunch, CoStar hosted an engaging information session where members learned about the company’s mission, culture, and early career opportunities. The session provided valuable insights into what it’s like to work at CoStar and what students can look forward to in the tech industry. Next, a Q&A panel gave WICS members the chance to hear candid advice and day-to-day insights directly from CoStar employees. The visit concluded with a tour of the Irvine office, offering a behind-the-scenes look at CoStar’s collaborative and innovative workspace. Overall, it was a fantastic afternoon filled with learning, networking, and career inspiration!

Spring 2025 Week 4: WICS x Crowdstrike
This Monday, April 21, 2025, WICS hosted a panel featuring industry professionals from CrowdStrike. The event began with panelist introductions and a sneak peek into CrowdStrike’s extensive internship program. Next, the representatives talked about a day in the life in their current role, giving WICS members insight into various tech careers. Panelists utilized PollEv to keep the event interactive and personalized, asking members for their opinions on things like favorite programming languages and CrowdStrike merch ideas. Finally, the event ended with a live Q&A for members to get answers to any remaining questions.

Spring 2025 Week 4: WICSlympics
On Friday, April 25, 2025, WICS hosted its annual WICS Olympics at Aldrich Park, bringing together mentorship families for an afternoon of fun, friendly competition, and team bonding. The event kicked off with a lively game of Red Light, Green Light, followed by a high-energy relay race featuring activities like three-legged races, bottle flipping, and more. The grand finale was a trivia showdown, where participants tested their knowledge on computer science and Disney Channel classics. Teams that earned the highest scores not only won exciting prizes but also gained valuable points toward the ongoing mentorship family competition.

Spring 2025 Week 5: Presentation Roulette
This Monday, April 28, 2025, WICS hosted a presentation night where club members grouped up to create slides on the topic of their choice. Once completed, each group’s slides were then passed over to another random group to blindly present. The creativity of every group really shined during this event, with topics ranging from “alphabet pasta rankings” to “lecture halls as songs.” With the stress of midterm season, a night of laughter and chaotic fun might’ve been just what the club members needed.

Spring 2025 Week 6: Hash Mash
On Monday, May 5, 2025, WICS partnered with BlockChain @ UCI to host an interactive blockchain-themed puzzle night in DBH 6011. Attendees were split into small teams and challenged to solve a series of logic-based puzzles and technical challenges designed to introduce key blockchain concepts in a fun, collaborative environment. From decoding messages to simulating transactions, each activity built on teamwork and critical thinking. The event wrapped up with a final round of high-stakes problem-solving, and the top-performing team earned a prize for their effort and creativity.

Spring 2025 Week 6: Sculpt and Study
On Wednesday, May 7, 2025, WICS hosted a study session, providing a quiet space where members could be productive and get creative. For the first hour of the study session, people chose from a variety of colors of air dry clay and sculpted cute little animals, trinkets, and characters. From giraffes, minions, frogs, and much more, there was an endless showcase of the members’ creative abilities! The remainder of the time was spent as an opportunity for members to study and work on their assignments.

Winter 2025 Week 1: Vision Board & Beyond
On Monday, January 6th, 2025, WICS welcomed everyone back from Winter break by informing attendees about WICS, such as what the organization is and some of the upcoming events. Additionally, members had the opportunity to manifest their ideal futures digitally or physically through a fun session of creating vision boards for the new year! While some people crafted BINGO cards and collages, others curated slideshows, Pinterest boards, and even Spotify playlists. Overall, everyone had a blast unleashing their creativity and catching up with friends, and it was a great way to kick off Winter quarter!

Winter 2025 Week 2: Breaking Barriers: Navigating Imposter Syndrome with Northrop Grumman x WICS
On Monday, January 13th during Week 2, WICS collaborated with Northrop Grumman for an event discussing imposter syndrome and how to navigate it. Emily King, Talent Acquisition Business Partner at NG, presented about imposter syndrome and introduced some panelists who were working full-time at Northrop Grumman. Members then had the opportunity to ask questions to the panelists, gaining insights on how to combat imposter syndrome and the support that NG offers to its employees. Afterwards, participants broke out into groups to discuss and share experiences with imposter syndrome, and shared their reflections with the whole room.

Winter 2025 Week 4: WICS x ACM Mock Technical Interview Prep
On Monday, January 27, 2025, WICS and ACM collaborated on an event allowing students to practice their technical interviewing skills in a collaborative environment. ACM started the event by presenting valuable advice on navigating technical interviews. Members then worked in teams through varying difficulties of LeetCode questions practicing these skills. Teams were scored on collaboration, correctness, efficiency, and communication for each question and the top three teams were rewarded with stickers, mouse pads, goodie bags, and referrals for the Optiver Women in Tech and Trading Insight Days.

Winter 2025 Week 6: Women at Microsoft
On February 10, WICS invited Jane Bove, Principal Program Manager at Microsoft, and Maureen Cotton, Co-founder of Integrated 8(a) Solutions, to share their career experiences. They led a thoughtful discussion about core values and work styles, guiding WICS members through a self assessment of what they’re naturally skilled at, especially in professional environments. Students shared stories of why they matched into Vision, Action, Empathy, or Analytical groups, as well as which core values resonated with them after a self reflection. The presenters concluded the event by emphasizing how important it is that students find a career that aligns with their values.

Winter 2025 Week 7: Mock Technical Interviews
On February 19, 2025, WICS partnered with companies including Microsoft, CrowdStrike, CoreLogic, CoStar, Fincons Group, SAP, and General Atomics to host mock technical and behavioral interviews for students. The event provided attendees with the opportunity to practice their interview skills in Software Engineering, Data Science, UI/UX, Product Management, and behavioral interviews with industry professionals while receiving personalized feedback. Participants gained valuable insights into technical problem-solving strategies and behavioral interview techniques, helping them better prepare for future job and internship opportunities. The event fostered a supportive environment where attendees could refine their skills, boost their confidence, and build meaningful industry connections!

Winter 2025 Week 9: WICS x The Dev Difference
On March 3rd, WICS invited Jovanay Carter, Jolene Bernagene, and Moyosore Okeremi, cofounders of The Dev Difference, to host a hands-on workshop featuring their cutting-edge AI mock interview platform. The session began with a discussion on interview preparation, followed by the presenters demonstrating how to navigate The Dev Difference platform. Members learned how to get personalized interview questions tailored to specific careers, companies, and job descriptions they are interested in. Members then had the opportunity to use the platform to practice behavioral and technical questions and receive real-time feedback from the AI. The event concluded with a raffle where six attendees won a premium subscription to The Dev Difference!

Winter 2025 Week 9: WICS x The Dev Difference
On March 3rd, WICS invited Jovanay Carter, Jolene Bernagene, and Moyosore Okeremi, cofounders of The Dev Difference, to host a hands-on workshop featuring their cutting-edge AI mock interview platform. The session began with a discussion on interview preparation, followed by the presenters demonstrating how to navigate The Dev Difference platform. Members learned how to get personalized interview questions tailored to specific careers, companies, and job descriptions they are interested in. Members then had the opportunity to use the platform to practice behavioral and technical questions and receive real-time feedback from the AI. The event concluded with a raffle where six attendees won a premium subscription to The Dev Difference!

Fall 2024 Week 5: SheTech Navigating Software Careers
This Monday, October 28th, 2024, WICS hosted an interactive SheTech workshop, giving members a glimpse into the world of software careers. The event started with introductions and icebreakers, followed by career advice from CoreLogic industry professionals. The highlight was an Agile-inspired Retrospective activity, where attendees used color-coded sticky notes to discuss successes, improvements, and challenges in their lives. Working in groups, participants shared insights and even got an inside look at Corelogic’s internships for Summer 2025. The workshop left members inspired and better prepared for their journeys in tech!

Fall 2024 Week 10: WICS x The HUB: AWS Certification Workshop
On Monday December 2, 2024, WICS collabed with The Hub to host an informational meeting about how to kickstart your journey to get AWS (Amazon Web Services) certified. We first started with hearing from a Solutions Architect at AWS, Trinity Key. She shared her story as a first year student at the University of Pittsburgh, to joining a lab at UCI, to her current position at AWS. She shared some key insights like finding a mentor and joining in current industry trends. Afterwards, AWS ambassadors from The HUB, Maithy and Tyler, gave a presentation on AWS certifications and resources to prepare for the certification exam. Finally, the table groups played a fun game of Jeopardy with topics of AWS, cloud computing, and etc. Overall, it was a fun night of getting to learn more about AWS and how to get certified. Thank you to The HUB, the AWS ambassadors and Trinity for coming out to this event!

Week 7: WICS x Hack present: Hacker Hangouts: Brainstorming Big Ideas
On Monday, May 13th, 2024, of Week 7, WICS hosted a collaboration meeting with Hack at UCI in preparation for the upcoming VenusHacks! Members from both clubs as well as anyone interested or planning on participating in VenusHacks came out to get more of an inside scoop on the upcoming hack-a-thon and to brainstorm with one another. After a quick icebreaker to get to know the members of each group, the facilitators started introducing example topics one at a time. After each one, each group brainstormed ideas for what kind of product they would create. After going through a couple topics, it was time for the groups to pitch their ideas to one another. This was a great opportunity to not only receive input on their ideas but also to practice presenting an idea! This night was filled with some great discussions and creativity. Next up: VenusHacks!

Spring 2024 Week 8: Super Study Sesh with Hero Cosmetics!
On Wednesday, May 22nd, 2024, WICS hosted a delightful study session in collaboration with Hero Cosmetics. Members gathered with textbooks and laptops to prepare for upcoming exams and projects. The session offered a mix of focused study time and interactive group discussions, allowing participants to delve deep into their coursework. The highlight of the event was Hero Cosmetics’ generous contribution, which included complimentary goodie bags filled with skincare products, including their popular Mighty Patch for blemishes. The attendees greatly appreciated the goodie bags, which offered a rejuvenating moment of self-care amidst their busy academic schedules. This thoughtful gesture helped create a perfect balance between hard work and self-care, leaving attendees feeling both accomplished and rejuvenated.

Several WICS mentorship families attended to enjoy a picnic at the beach, play games, and meet new friends. This was a great opportunity to break the ice and relax before finals.

Fall 2024 Week 1: First General Meeting
Welcome to our new students and welcome back to everyone else! This past Monday, we kicked off the school year with WICS’ first general meeting, where over 100 students joined us to learn about the organization and our various committees. Fall 2024 Week 2: Mentorship Mixer
This Monday, October 7, 2024, WICS welcomed a stunning 130 participants to our annual Mentorship Mixer! Our room was teaming with eager potential mentors and mentees, some returning from previous years of mentorship and many hoping to join for the first time. The mentorship theme of the year, Disney Channel Original Series and Movies, was revealed and met with happy nostalgia. However, the real icing on the cake was the rounds of icebreaker questions quickly turning into insightful conversations across the room. We loved seeing everyone get to know each other better throughout the night, and can’t wait to see who our final mentorship family matches are at the Week 4 Mentorship Reveal!! xoxo

Fall 2024 Week 3: WICS Games
On Monday, October 14, 2024, during Week 3, WICS had the privilege of inviting twelve company representatives to participate in WICS Games with our members! Each representative was assigned to a team who collaborated to solve three rounds of questions to the best of their abilities: Computer Science trivia, logical puzzles, and coding problems. This gave the girls an opportunity to talk to women already in the working field who could share their experiences on undergraduate schooling and internships. It was an amazing competitive experience against the clock while making connections with their peers and company representatives!

Fall 2024 Week 3: Study Session/Mentorship Mixer Pt.2
On Wednesday of Week 3, WICS hosted a mentorship event blended with a study session. The event kicked off with introductions, giving mentors and mentees a chance to share a bit about themselves and their journeys in tech. After the introductions, the group dove into the study session, with mentors offering guidance and insight while working alongside their mentees. It was a great opportunity for those who couldn’t attend last week’s mentorship mixer or for anyone who needed more time to build connections and decide on their ideal mentor or mentee. We’re excited to see the final mentorship family matches at the Week 4 Mentorship Reveal!

Spring 2024 Week 1: First General Meeting
On Monday, April 1, we had the first general meeting of Spring Quarter, where frequent WICS-goers mingled with plenty of newcomers. At the first meeting of each quarter, we always try to provide attendees an overview of the WICS: who we are, what we do, and, of course, what the WICS URL is. This time around, though, we added an element of competitive fun to our information session. Attendees were placed in groups of 6-7 people around the perimeter of the room. After getting to know the other members of their group through a mix of typical and unconventional icebreaker questions, the competition began! A spinner was used to randomly pick a question, some personal and others about WICS. One person from each group stood in the middle of their group and was tasked with quickly finding someone who could answer the question. They then ran with that person to a chair at the center of the room, winning a point for their team if they sat down first and answered the question correctly. While seeing everyone charge toward the center of the room the moment a question appeared on the screen could be intimidating, the reward was well worth it. Every pair who answered a question was entered in a raffle. At the end, a name was drawn, and the winner got a brand new water bottle!

Spring 2024 Week 2: WICS x WiT Coffee Chats
On Monday, April 8, 2023, WICS held WiT Coffee Chats, a collaboration event with WiT (Women in Technology). Members and WiT representatives teamed up in groups of 2-4 to play a quick icebreaker of Wiki search where each group started at the C++ Wiki page and had to get to the coffee wiki page using only the hyperlinks. After the icebreaker, the co-chairs of WiT, Jennifer and Julie, gave a quick introduction to WiT and their goals. For the remainder of the event, members rotated tables for a total of 4 rounds to ask WiT members any of their burning questions about tech, being a women in tech, etc.

Spring 2024 Week 2: Puzzle Relay / Study Session + Deloitte Talk
On Wednesday, April 10, 2024, of Week 2, WICS hosted a Study Session, Deloitte Talk, and Puzzle Relay Mentorship event. WICS members spent the first section of the event studying while enjoying catered Mendocino Farms. Next, representatives from Deloitte shared a presentation outlining one of their software engineering projects: accelerating FDA submissions by digitizing clinical trial documentation and leveraging GenAI. Towards the end of the event, both mentorship members and general members participated in an exciting puzzle relay, competing in folding origami, solving riddles, and completing word searches in order to win prizes!

Spring 2024 Week 2: NetWICS
On Saturday, April 13, 2024, WICS organized a career-centered event for high school girls interested in computer science field to come together and meet each other as well as UCI undergraduates. After 2 ice breakers and an introduction, attendees were divided into 5 teams to participate in the shark tank activity. Each team was required to build a 10-minutes slides to pitch at the end, and they have approximately 35 minutes to complete. After the activity, participants were able to collaborate with others to learn pitching skill. Lunch was provided after the previous activity. To continue the event, WICS presented some tips before attendees participate in mock interview activity. The semi-formal interview with mentors allowed the students to have more experience and learn more from the mentors and also other students. To wrapped up the day, there was a Q&A section followed by feedback time and scavenger hunt game. The event, in general, allows students to connect and network with other students from different area and also undergraduates at UCI who are learning in the field of ICS.

Spring 2024 Week 3: Girl Boss and Girl Budget with WICS
In Week 3, WICS hosted an event titled “Girl Boss and Girl Budget,” focusing on financial literacy and empowerment. The event covered Budgeting Basics, including topics such as debt management, savings strategies, and the 50/30/20 Rule for budgeting. Participants also learned about managing loans, credit cards, and the differences between checking and saving accounts. Additionally, the event discussed investment accounts, including stocks and retirement funds, providing valuable insights for attendees to take control of their financial future.

Spring 2024 Week 4: Startups in Tech with Manifest
This Monday, April 22, 2024 at 6:30PM in DBH 6011, WICS held their first ever collaboration with Manifest, an entrepreneur club here at UCI. During this meeting, we learned more about some of the members of Manifest’s journey in entrepreneurship as well as their advice on how to get into the business of entrepreneurship through an open panel. We also had the chance to pitch our own silly start up ideas after being given two random objects and try selling it the panel. It was hilarious and a very informative event. We look forward to working with them again!

Spring 2024 Week 4: Startups in Tech with Manifest
This Monday, April 22, 2024 at 6:30PM in DBH 6011, WICS held their first ever collaboration with Manifest, an entrepreneur club here at UCI. During this meeting, we learned more about some of the members of Manifest’s journey in entrepreneurship as well as their advice on how to get into the business of entrepreneurship through an open panel. We also had the chance to pitch our own silly start up ideas after being given two random objects and try selling it the panel. It was hilarious and a very informative event. We look forward to working with them again!

Spring 2024 Week 5: Committee Spotlight – Community Outreach
On Monday, April 29th, 2024 of Week 5, WICS featured the Outreach Committee and the work they have been doing for this past year. Everything from NetWics, to a WebDev bootcamps, to a Scratch-a-Thon, Outreach reached 65 participants consisting of middle and high schoolers across 4 different events. Once the members got to learn more about what Outreach does and how to get involved, they participated in a Kahoot trivia game and the top winners received WICS limited edition merch! Afterwards, those who were interested in joining the Outreach Committee got the chance to have coffee chat conversations with current outreach members, asking questions about what the event planning process looks like and how to apply for the next season of committee applications. During these conversations, members also got to enjoy the rest of the night making bracelets and keychains, a perfect way to destress in the midst of midterms!

Spring 2024 Week 5: GHC Info Session / Study Session
On Wednesday, May 1st, 2024, WICS hosted an information session about the Grace Hopper Celebration (GHC) and our GHC scholarship application. We began with an overview of the event logistics and application process, followed by an insightful Q&A session with a panel of four individuals who attended GHC in 2023. The event concluded with a productive 1.5-hour study session, where members collaborated and supported each other while preparing for mid-terms.

Spring 2024 Week 6: WICS x CTC General Meeting
On Monday, May 6, 2024, during Week 6,WICS had the privilege of teaming up with Commit the Change (CTC) at UCI to organize a React workshop. This workshop centered on introducing students to the fundamentals of web development using the React framework, guiding them through the creation of a user interface. Additionally, participants engaged in a fun game of Kahoot to compete using their React skills and concluded the event with an exciting coding challenge!

Winter 2024 Week 5: WICS x ACM Mock Technical Interview Prep
On Monday, February 5, 2024, WICS held a mock technical interview prep event in collaboration with ACM. In this competition styled event, members worked in groups to solve coding questions similar to those asked in technical interviews. There were 3 rounds, and each round tackled different coding concepts. In the end, groups were scored on efficiency as well as accuracy, and the top groups received prizes. Through this event, members were able to practice their technical skills, collaborate with others, and also know the expectations of a technical interview!

Winter 2024 Week 6: WICS x SWE Valentine’s Social
This Monday, February 12, 2024, WICS hosted a Valentines-themed social in collaboration with the Society of Women Engineers (SWE). Members sat in chairs around the room to make cards and goodie bags for their favorite people. Some people used ribbons and googly eyes to creatively decorate their cards, and others wrote cheesy pick up lines or cute puns. The goodie bags were filled with candy and cute stickers. There was also a photo wall for members to take pictures in front of.

On Wednesday, February 14, 2024, during Week 6, WICS hosted a lovely mentorship event in celebration of Valentine’s Day! Participants immersed themselves in various creative activities to ignite the spirit of affection. From exploring watercolor painting to crafting clever pick-up lines for their valentines/galentines, and enjoying delicious snacks and refreshing drinks, the event was a perfect creative outlet and bonding experience. After the fun activities, the two-hour study session provided a space for members to work together and help each other with their academic pursuits.

Winter 2024 Week 7: Mock Technical Interviews
On Tuesday, February 21, 2024, brought 28 WICS members and 22 company interviewers together for the WICS Mock Technical Interviews! From 9:00 am to 5:00 pm members attended one-on-one 45-minute virtual sessions with company recruiters, simulating the technical interview environment. Each of the 40+ interviews with software engineering, product management, UX/UI, or behavioral questions, provided participants with tailored feedback on their performance. With newfound confidence and connections to recruiters, interviewees are now ready to succeed in their career pursuits!

Winter 2024 Week 8: WICS x GDSC Introduction to Recommendation Systems
On Monday, February 26, 2024, of Week 8, WICS collaborated with GDSC to teach their members how recommendation systems are made. GDSC’s members introduced the mathematics behind finding a user’s interests based on their current preferences. They then showed how the math is implemented into code to streamline the grunt-work of the calculations. Finally, members participated in a challenging and competitive Kahoot game covering all the topics that were taught!

Winter 2024 Week 8: Crepe Boothing
This Tuesday, February 27, 2024, WICS held a Crepe Boothing event from 11:00am-2pm on the outer Ring Road to help fundraise for the club and to promote what WICS is. People can get 1 crepe for $3 or get a bang for their buck and get 2 for $5. Customers were also able to select the filling that they wanted (Nutella, Biscoff) as well as some toppings (strawberries, chocolate drizzle, caramel drizzle, etc). Not only was it nice to meet new people but it was also a delicious treat to have afterwards! We hope you enjoyed!

Winter 2024 Week 9: WICS x CoStar: How to be a Confident Woman in Tech
On Monday, March 4, 2024, WICS collaborated with CoStar Group and held a presentation on how to recognize personal strengths and apply them as a woman in the tech industry. Members were challenged to evaluate their own technical and problem solving skills, and they were encouraged with tips on how to become more confident in their own abilities. They also learned the do’s and don’ts in an interview and how to stand out during the interview process. The event ended with a Q&A session with a representative from CoStar Group, where members were able to gain more insight about what recruiters look for when they are hiring.

Winter 2024 Week 9: Study Session / WICSdunnit: The Great Mentorship Mystery
On Wednesday, March 6th, 2024, WICS organized an exciting mentorship event centered around a mystery and puzzle-solving challenge! The event revolved around the mysterious disappearance of the WICS key, tasking members with uncovering the details of the incident. To solve the mystery, members formed teams to deduce the “Who,” “Where,” and “Why” behind the story. Through collaborative effort, they tackled various puzzle clues, ultimately cracking the WICSdunnit case! Following the mentorship event, participants dedicated time to a two-hour study session, diligently focusing on their coursework as the academic quarter draws to a close.

The event revolved around the mysterious disappearance of the WICS key, tasking members with uncovering the details of the incident. To solve the mystery, members formed teams to deduce the “Who,” “Where,” and “Why” behind the story. Through collaborative effort, they tackled various puzzle clues, ultimately cracking the WICSdunnit case! Following the mentorship event, participants dedicated time to a two-hour study session, diligently focusing on their coursework as the academic quarter draws to a close.

On Friday, March 8, 2024, WICS brought together mentorship families and members for a delightful Mentorship Hide-and-Go Seek event! The event kicked off with an explanation of the rules, and soon after, teams scattered throughout Aldrich Park, strategically hiding and seeking their way to victory. After the excitement of the game, everyone gathered to indulge in delicious Costco pie and other snacks. The evening culminated in a screening of the movie, Mamma Mia, bringing the perfect blend of fun, food, and film to the event.

Fall 2023 Week 6: Scratch-a-thon
On November 11th and 12th, WICS Community Outreach hosted a Scratch-a-thon for 5th to 8th graders from OCEAA and El Sol Science and Arts Academy. On the first day, students got to learn the basics of Scratch, covering topics from motion and looks to control and sensors to operators and variables. On the second day, they were able to use what they learned to create their own Scratch project in groups. At the end, we had 4 winning teams that were able to bring back prizes.

Fall 2023 Week 7: API Workshop with Postman Pt. 1
On Saturday, November 18th, 2023 of Fall 2023, WICS held an API workshop with Postman. Led by WICS members, Tiffany Cheung and Sophia Shaw, participants learned all about the basics of APIs and the world’s leading API platform, Postman. Upon arrival, participants enjoyed a breakfast buffet from IHOP before jumping into the workshop. The hosts walked participants through how to register for Postman’s API Fundamentals Student Expert course and through the first half of the modules. After completing the course, participants will not only be a student expert on using Postman, but will also receive an official Postman certification and a swag bag!

Fall 2023 Week 8: DATA X WICS Speaker Session: Women i7:3 AI/ML
On Monday, November 20, 2023, WICS and Data @ UCI hosted a speaker panel to showcase four accomplished women in the field of artificial intelligence (AI) and machine learning (ML). The panelists — Kate Deyneka, Nadia Ahmed, Fulya Ozcan, and Shringar Sharan — captivated the audience through sharing their invaluable insights in the evolving field of AI. The event highlighted their unique, personal journeys throughout their careers and the challenges they overcame along the way. Attendees were encouraged to ask questions and connect with the panelists. The event not only celebrated the achievements of these exceptional women but also served as a catalyst for fostering a more inclusive and diverse community within the tech industry. Attendees left with the knowledge and inspiration to navigate the rapidly evolving field of AI/ML.

Fall 2023 Week 9: Pitch Perfect with Pacific Life
On Monday, November 27, 2023, WICS partnered with company Pacific Life and held a workshop focused on developing presentation and public speaking skills in professional settings. Representatives from Pacific Life gave insightful information on general speaking tips, using the PAR (Project, Action, Result) method, and exercising nonverbal communication. Attendees were subsequently divided into groups to practice these skills in an engaging way by preparing a two-minute presentation that solved a workplace problem.
