Winter 2024 Week 3: WICS InternQuest
On Monday, January 22nd, 2024 of Week 3, WICS hosted an InternQuest Session to help members develop a better understanding of how to land a summer internship. A quick presentation and examples were given on the importance and strategy of building a strong resume and Linkedin. Members were then paired up into smaller groups where they engaged in giving constructive feedback to each other for their respective resumes and Linkedin profiles. Closing off the night, members were encouraged to expand their Linkedin network by connecting with one another. Ultimately, it was a night of learning and helping members take a step closer towards landing their dream summer internship.

On Monday, January 22nd, 2024 of Week 3, WICS hosted an InternQuest Session to help members develop a better understanding of how to land a summer internship. A quick presentation and examples were given on the importance and strategy of building a strong resume and Linkedin. Members were then paired up into smaller groups where they engaged in giving constructive feedback to each other for their respective resumes and Linkedin profiles. Closing off the night, members were encouraged to expand their Linkedin network by connecting with one another. Ultimately, it was a night of learning and helping members take a step closer towards landing their dream summer internship.

Winter 2024 Week 4: WICS x VGDC Mad Pitch
On Monday, January 29, 2024, WICS collaborated with Video Game Design Club (VGDC) to host a fun competition where teams would work together to pitch an idea for a new game. To make things more interesting, members were to fill in provided MadLibs templates, without knowing what their new game would be inspired by. Keeping in mind the many aspects with game design, such as art, design, and storytelling, each group used their creativity to produce an exciting presentation. By the end of the event, all teams pitched their innovative games, with the best ones winning notebooks, T-shirts, and more!

Fall 2023 Week 4: NSBE x SHPE x WICS Networking Dinner Night
On Tuesday, October 24, 2023, SHPE, WICS, and NSBE hosted a successful Networking Dinner Night from 6:30pm to 8:30pm at Donald Bren Hall (DBH) Room 6011. The event brought together 10 prominent companies, allowing attendees to network with potential recruiters. Participating companies such as TEC, Corelogic, Cordoba Corporation, and more attended. Those who secured a spot at this exclusive event had the opportunity to connect and explore new career prospects. It was a fantastic evening of networking and professional growth!

Fall 2023 Week 5: Beyond Handshakes: How to Network with General Atomics x WICS
This Monday, October 30, 2023, WICS held a How To Network session with General Atomics! It started with information about General Atomics, what they do, and their internship program. General Atomics representatives also discussed talking with companies and tools that are often used by students to network with other companies. In addition, they discussed the importance of highlighting who YOU are as an applicant, what you bring to the table and what makes you unique. Activities such as presenting your “elevator pitch” also gave insight into sharpening our pitch to recruiters, etc. Everyone had a great time and learned from the experience of the General Atomics representatives.

Week 2: Build Your Own Website with Git
On Friday, April 14, 2023, WICS held its third Build Your Own Website (BYOW) workshop. This session was focused on the use of Git and GitHub. Attendees were taught what git what and the basics of git. After, they were given the opportunity to do it on their own with the guidance of those who planned the event and other helpers. Git is an important skill for those who plan on entering the programming field, so many left the event with a very important skill for them to apply in their classes and eventually in the workforce!

Spring 2023 Week 3: Community Outreach x El Sol Scratch-a-thon
On the weekend of April 15-16, 2023, WICS Outreach Committee hosted a 2-day Scratch event in collaboration with El Sol Academy’s elementary and middle school girls. On the first day, our Outreach members taught our Scratch curriculum filled with interactive demos and code-alongs, and all our students and everyone were able to make short animations based on all the taught material such as the use of loops, costume changes, conditionals, etc. On Day 2, our students formed groups of two and worked, with the help of WICS mentors, on creating their own projects from start to finish using Scratch within the duration of the event hackathon-style. At the end of the day, each pair demoed and presented their projects to everyone, with three teams winning various prizes sponsored by the UCI Office of Access and Inclusion.

Winter 2023 Week 7: WICS x General Atomics Women in Tech Panel
On Wednesday, February 22, 2023, WICS held an Informational Session & Women in Tech Panel with General Atomics Aeronautical System (GA). GA is a defense and diversified technologies company that is a world leader in creating Unmanned Aircraft Systems. These aircraft are remotely piloted and are useful for military, security, and environmental operations to ensure the safety of individuals in firefighting, search, and rescue operations. After learning more about their company, the software engineers of General Atomics discussed software development roles and the rewarding experiences in creating these aircraft. They answered various questions about preparing for their career and advised students who aspire to enter the same industry. They mentioned a summer internship opportunity that would require a 10-week commitment in San Diego that focuses on opportunities for professional development, mentorship, networking, and technical lectures.

Winter 2023 Week 8: WICS x Taco Bell
On Monday, February 27th, 2023, WICS hosted an event for Taco Bell Company representatives who gave their pieces on how Taco Bell serves up innovation. The company combines in-store kitchen technology with the cloud. Some of the representatives were UC Irvine alumni and offered their career paths and the steps to land a job at Taco Bell. Near the end, a Q&A session was offered and allowed attendees to ask informative and fun questions for the representatives to answer. The questions were even brought back and had attendees answering as well. It was an overall engaging and spec-TACO-lar event for all!

Winter 2023: Community Outreach Cypress Bootcamp
During the winter quarter, WICS’ Outreach Committee worked with Cypress College and Magnolia High School students to embark on a 3-day boot camp filled with technical workshops, professional development, and team-building activities. The cohort of 11 girls learned a variety of skills deep-divided into topics that were completely new to them.
Day 1 was held on January 28, where Outreach taught the basics of HTML and CSS, resulting in a final project of a recipe webpage. Day 2 on February 4 covered Game Development, where the students learned how to build a text-based game using Twine, and explored topics such as if-else statements, conditionals, variables, and incorporating CSS into the look of the game. The final day on February 11 was held at UCI. The outreach committee gave a tour of UCI with some very obscure fun facts and then proceeded to lead a professional development lesson about how to use the STAR method during interviews. The day concluded with a Shark Tank competition where the students crafted together a product using cardboard, construction paper, and other art materials, and pitched it to the audience. The winning product was a box that acted as a vending machine to release boxes with encouraging and heartfelt messages inside of them.

Winter 2023 Week 9: Movie Night
On Monday, March 6th, 2023, WICS hosted a movie night, where members were able to relax and watch the recent Puss in Boots movie, Puss in Boots: The Last Wish. It was a pleasant break from the schoolwork, and as we near the end of the quarter, a great motivation to end off the quarter strong. The light-hearted thrill and childish humor paired with some heart-touching moments really made the movie the right pick to de-stress. Members even stayed a bit later than the usual ending time to finish off the movie!

Spring 2023 Week 1: First General Meeting + UTC Social Event
On Monday, April 3, 2023, WICS held a general meeting to start off the spring quarter. Members were able to learn more about WICS and the different opportunities and events happening throughout the quarter. Then, all the members played Never Ending Story where each person said a word one at a time to create a story. After that, the members played one more game called Rock Paper Scissors Showdown. For this, two members would first do introductions, then play Rock Paper Scissors, and the winner will play against another winner until there is one person left. These games provided a fun experience for the members and were a great way for them to bond! To close off the night, WICS went to UTC to socialize and get to know each other better!

Spring 2023 Week 2: Company Cookie Decorating Contest
On Monday, April 10, 2023, WICS held its first-ever cookie decorating contest with over 50 participants. Members were split into groups and assigned companies from WICS’s 2022-2023 sponsors, where they learned more about the company’s culture and shared creative ways how to represent that on a cookie. With all the creative designs, the competition was tight, but ultimately Taco Bell’s group came out top. Overall, the event served as a fun way to learn more about WICS’s generous sponsors and encourage more members to socialize.

Spring 2023 Week 2: Company Cookie Decorating Contest
On Monday, April 10, 2023, WICS held its first-ever cookie decorating contest with over 50 participants. Members were split into groups and assigned companies from WICS’s 2022-2023 sponsors, where they learned more about the company’s culture and shared creative ways how to represent that on a cookie. With all the creative designs, the competition was tight, but ultimately Taco Bell’s group came out top. Overall, the event served as a fun way to learn more about WICS’s generous sponsors and encourage more members to socialize.

Fall 2022 Week 10: WICSGiving Potluck
On Monday, November 28 2022, the WICS community came together to celebrate WICSgiving and give thanks for the year that has gone by. Members brought a wide array of contributions to the potluck feast, which was made extra special by the revival of homemade WICS crepes. Everyone partook in activities like ‘Pin the Hat on the Turkey’ and sharing the things they were grateful for. The food, games and people made this event the perfect closing off to the quarter.

Winter 2023 Week 1: First General Meeting
On Monday, January 9, 2023, WICS kicked off the Winter Quarter with the first general meeting of 2023. New and familiar faces learned more about the mission statement of WICS, the board, committee members, and upcoming events. After the presentation, members played a game to discover similarities and differences among themselves, with two lucky people winning exclusive swag. To end off the night, members socialized and played board games to bond, letting their friendships warm their hearts.

Winter 2023 Week 1: Committee Retreat
This Saturday, January 14 to Sunday, January 15, WICS hosted a retreat for committee members for the 2022-2023 school year. After all committee members arrived at the retreat, they participated in team bonding activities such as a huge game of telephone, Sardines, and more! Throughout the retreat, committee members also made each other small notes of kindness and appreciation to take home. Committee members bonded over both dinner and breakfast as they enjoyed yummy meals! As the first in-person retreat in several years, this year’s committee retreat ended on a high note after a weekend filled with bonding and positivity!

Winter 2023 Week 3: ACM x WICS MTI Technical Interview Prep Workshop
This Monday, January 23, WICS and ACM hosted a collaborative event in which participants worked in groups with rotating proctors to practice solving technical interview problems together. Split into three 18-minute rounds, each round had the proctor at each table present a problem and give feedback as the participants discussed and proposed solutions. From this event, participants were able to practice their technical as well as communication skills both between themselves as well as with the proctors.

Winter 2023 Week 3: Mock Technical Interviews
On Wednesday, January 25, 2023, WICS held its Mock Technical Interviews event over Zoom. Employees — from companies Cisco, CrowdStrike, Expedia, Google, Northrop Grumman, Slalom, Taco Bell, and Uber — were invited to give students mock interviews. Attendees had the chance to practice their technical interview skills and interact 1-on-1 with real company representatives. It was a great opportunity for them to gain experience in the hiring process, receive helpful feedback in a more casual setting, and get any career questions they had answered by people actually working in the industry. Throughout the day, WICS committee members worked in shifts to assign breakout rooms, check people in and out at their designated times, and keep everything running smoothly.

Winter 2023 Week 4: Mentorship Field Day
On Friday, February 3, 2023, of Week 4, WICS held a Mentorship Field Day in Aldrich Park. Mentors and mentees met up to play games and earn points for their mentorship families. The event started with Love My Neighbor, where mentors and mentees formed a circle and switched places with each other based on their answer to the question “I love my neighbor who…”. Next, each mentorship family formed a line to play Charades Telephone. A game similar to regular Telephone, but with silent acting instead of whispering. The even ended with a campus-wide scavenger hunt. Mentorship families were given a list of riddles, and had 15 minutes to run to take pictures in as many of the places that the riddles described as they could. The family with the most pictures won the game, but all participating families got points for their efforts.

Winter 2023 Week 5: Intro to Cybersecurity with Cyber@UCI
On February 6th, 2023, WICS collaborated with Cyber@UCI to present an introductory cybersecurity workshop. Students learned the basics of cybersecurity and were given some tips to get started. The workshop began with a follow-along demonstration, as everyone worked together to hack a website (with specific permission to do so!). Upon successful hacking, workshop participants broke into small groups to try to hack another website. WICS members and the presenters walked around to offer hints and answer questions throughout the small group activity. There was lots of chatter and cheers of success throughout the night as participants put their new skills to the test!

Fall 2022 Week 6: Mentorship Reveal
This Monday, October 31, 2022, WICS hosted a spooktacular Mentorship Reveal! Embracing the Halloween spirit, DBH 6011 was decked out in cobwebs and caution tape, and a WICS-original Halloween playlist completed the vibe. Many attendees came dressed in their Halloween costumes, ranging from Inosuke from Demon Slayer to Dora the Explorer, to get a head start on earning points for their mentorship family. At the beginning of the meeting, attendees received a card with their name on one side and an unknown food item on the other, and they had to ask one another yes-or-no questions in an attempt to find out what food item was written on their card. Those who had the same food were a mentor-mentee pairing, and those with food items from the same fast-food restaurant were in the same mentorship family! After everyone found their groupings, each family was tasked with creating a poster and pitch for a new Halloween-themed menu item for their restaurant. Each family had fun coming up with unique ideas while they bonded with the new friends who will support them throughout the coming year!

Fall 2022 Week 6: Mentorship Social
This Friday, November 4, 2022, WICS hosted a Mentorship Social in which families within the mentorship program teamed up with other families to compete in several rounds of competitions to win family points. The first round was a fun trivia game in which people were able to test out their knowledge of other people’s fun facts. The second round was a team event where everyone in each team needed to line up in order based on the specified category without talking. The last round was an active event in which each team had a member run to find someone who fit the description listed and the person had to prove they fit the description. Overall, it was a super fun event where everyone was able to learn more about other mentorship family members!

This Monday, November 7, 2022, WICS invited Rivian university recruiter Sara Donilon to present a LinkedIn profile workshop. Spring 2022 Week 8: WICSino Night
WICS held a successful casino-themed game night on Monday, May 16, 2022. Classic casino games, like Blackjack and Slots, were mixed with other party games, like Uno and Bingo, to give members a chance to wager and win tickets to enter into our raffle. This was a great opportunity for WICS and the ICS community to come together to relax and have a good time, especially nearing finals. In the end, everyone walked out with pockets full of memories and fists full of pink raffle tickets.

Spring 2022 Week 9: WICSxPICS
On Tuesday, May 24th, 2022, WICS hosted a lunch with some professors of ICS! With warm welcomes of delicious sandwiches, soda pop, and some classic chip favorites, Professors Shindler, Wong-Ma, Navarro, and Pattis joined fellow students for some lunch. Some highlights include Professor Wong-Ma sharing her summer plans to travel to Disney World, Professor Shindler, being a good conversationalist, welcoming new students to join in on his group, Professor Navarro’s plans for a trip around Europe over the summer, Professor Pattis sharing “The Last Lecture” by Randy Pausch being one of his favorite books/lectures to learn about life advice. The event had students engaged in the professor’s stories and wisdom with the appearance of giggles here and there. Toward the end of the event, stories trailed the end while students continued to ask questions. Overall, the event successfully allowed students to learn more about their professors beyond the classroom and the professors to hear about what their students are interested in learning about them.

Spring 2022 Week 9: WICS Banquet
On Friday, May 27, 2022, WICS held a Banquet at BJ’s celebrating the end of the school year as well as the end to this year’s Mentorship Program. All WICS members–mentors, mentees, committee members, general members, and even some first-time WICS attendees–had the chance to socialize and relax before Week 10 and Finals week. In addition to eating yummy food, like salad, deep dish pizza, and spaghetti, WICS presented awards to Mentorship families. Goodie bags were gifted that contained prizes like light-up panda and dolphin figurines, and silly awards that members nominated each other for were presented. The Banquet ended with all members taking pictures in the sunset to commemorate the event and the wonderful memories made.

Summer 2022: Lunch with the Mayor
On Saturday, June 25, 2022, WICS members attended the “Lunch with the Mayor” event, hosted by Live Good. Live Good is a student-run, environmentally-friendly company in Irvine that focuses on education, diversity, and promoting social good through their business model, sustainable products, and humanitarian projects. During the event, WICS members met with the mayor of Irvine, Farrah Khan, along with other Irvine civic representatives, and shared lunch and conversation about contemporary topics which included the history and future of Irvine, transportation, city planning, and the careers of the attendees. Needless to say, our WICS representatives enjoyed the event and learned about the behind-the-scenes management of the city that they study in!

Fall 2022 Week 3: WICS Games
On Monday, October 10, 2022, over 100 people gathered in Donald Bren Hall for WICS Games 2022! This annual event brings company representatives and eager ICS students together for a night of riddles, coding questions, networking, and fun! The group was split up into 12 teams, who competed to solve their questions and earn points. Each team worked with their company representative to create a team name and chant, which they had to perform for everyone. Pasta, breadsticks, and salad fueled the participants as they solved the questions, with the help of their company representative and a WICS buddy. In the end, three teams came out on top and were awarded prizes. Shout-out to the representatives from Crowdstrike, Meta, CoStar Group, Slalom, and Northrop Grumman for volunteering their time to make WICS Games 2022 a success!

Spring 2022 Week 3: Movie Night
On Monday April 11th, 2022, WICS held a movie night social for members to hang out in a casual space and get to know each other. We watched the new Disney movie Turning Red which details a preteen girl’s attempts to go to a concert amidst her mom’s meddling and adorable red pandas. Members seemed to really enjoy the movie as it was so relatable and there was definitely a lot of laughter throughout the room. The movie had a really sweet message of being true to yourself and accepting who you are which we always promote here at WICS especially when entering new spaces like the tech industry!

Spring 2022 Week 4: Projects at Chipotle
On Monday April 18, 2022, WICS hosted the Projects at Chipotle event in partnership with Chipotle! WICS members joined together in person and connected with representatives from Chipotle through Zoom to dive deeper into the various projects Chipotle does, specifically Extras through the Chipotle app. There were representatives from the Program Management sector, Product Design sector, and Software Development sector, and each representative discussed their experiences and what they contribute to the team. Following the presentation by Chipotle, WICS members asked various questions, which were answered by the Chipotle representatives. Overall, the Projects at Chipotle session was an insightful look into how Chipotle takes on projects and executes them.

Spring 2022 Week 6: Mentorship MAYhem
On Friday, May 6, 2022, WICS held Mentorship MAYhem. At this event filled with entertaining games, light-hearted competition, and lots of laughter, WICS mentorship families were able to destress together after a busy few weeks of midterms and projects. The evening started out with a Quizizz on all things Nickelodeon, testing the members’ knowledge on SpongeBob, Big Time Rush, and everything in between. Families then went head-to-head in a game of telephone charades: only one person knew what the word was, and each person had to act out to the next in line what they thought the word could be. There were some expectedly wild guesses as everyone tried their best to act out words like “wedding” and “DoorDash”. To conclude the evening, everyone went out to the patio for snacks and one last game. Two families competed in Dijkstra’s Algorithm Tournament at a time, trying to find the correct path across the tiles as quickly as possible, while the third family watched from the sidelines and enjoyed Goldfish, Rice Krispies, and Fruit by the Foot.

Fall 2025 Week 7: AI@UCI x WICS Spot the Bot
On Monday, November 10, 2025, WICS collaborated with AI@UCI to host a fun and informative event where members learned more about the AI field and participated in interactive games! We started off with a presentation on influential women in AI as well as an overview of the basic steps of how an AI model is trained and tested. After receiving this brief introduction to the field, members participated in an Among Us-inspired game, working with each other to weed out the AI-generated response among their teammates’ human responses to a given prompt. The event concluded with a competitive Kahoot quiz on what we learned from the initial presentation, with members neck and neck down to the final question!

Fall 2025 Week 9: WICS x CoStar – Career Pathways in Tech
On Monday, November 24, 2025, WICS partnered with CoStar Group to host an informative session dedicated to exploring the recruitment process and day-to-day life in DevOps. CoStar representatives shared valuable tips on crafting strong resumes, preparing for interviews, and building the skills essential for success in DevOps roles. Throughout the event, WICS members had the chance to personally ask questions and receive tailored advice, making the session interactive, insightful, and especially meaningful for those interested in pursuing a career in DevOps. Overall, the event provided an excellent opportunity for students to gain a deeper understanding of what a future in DevOps might look like!

Winter 2026 Week 4: WICS x Google – Mock Technical Interview Prep
On Monday, January 26, 2026, WICS partnered with Google to host an interactive mock technical interview prep workshop. Members got to hear about Google’s internship and full-time recruitment process, including insights on behavioral and technical interviews, key topics to prepare for, and personal experiences from the Googlers. Attendees then broke into small groups, each paired with one of the five Googlers, to collaboratively work through a SWE-style technical interview problem while asking questions and receiving guidance. The workshop concluded with additional Q&A discussions within these groups, helping members gain confidence as they prepare for mock interviews in February.

https://photos.app.goo.gl/mb8QcnFi6daGiugM7

Winter 2026 Week 6: WICS x Cyber @ UCI
On Monday, February 9, 2026, during Week 6, WICS teamed up with Cyber@UCI to create a workshop on malware analysis. The workshop focused on introducing how malware functions in the real world, hiding in applications within computers. Attendees learned about the differences between static and dynamic analysis and then put their skills to the test by hunting down a goose-themed malicious program!

Winter 2026 Week 8: Budgeting Baddies 101
On Monday, February 23, 2026, WICS members learned to budget like baddies and reinforced what they learned with an interactive Game of Life, a budgeting game with the goal of finishing with the highest cumulative savings. Members battled and budgeted for their characters to keep their happiness up and their debt down, all while handling chaos events and the throes of life. The event was a great way to help members learn to handle finances while having fun!

Winter 2026 Week 10: WICStery Night
On Monday, March 9th, 2026, during Week 10, WICS wrapped up the winter quarter with a fun WICStery event where members worked together to solve the mystery of which professor stole our club pizza. During this activity, club members were revealed clues sequentially and given time to discuss with their teammates and form suspicions about who the culprit might be. Members also had the opportunity to share their suspicions and reasoning with the rest of the room. This activity allowed members to relax, unwind, and connect with one another while enjoying a lighthearted and interactive way to close out the quarter.

Spring 2026 Week 3: WICS x Teach For America
This Monday, April 13th, 2026, WICS had the pleasure of collaborating with Teach For America (TFA) to introduce opportunities for UCI students to help underserved children gain a fulfilling education. This presentation centered on the Ignite Fellowship and other roles that students can join within TFA, where they can directly tutor children around the country and make a real impact. Students also participated in a tutor and student scenario, acting out different learner types and figuring out how to effectively guide them through problems!

Spring 2026 Week 3: WICS x Teach For America
This Monday, April 13th, 2026, WICS had the pleasure of collaborating with Teach For America (TFA) to introduce opportunities for UCI students to help underserved children gain a fulfilling education. This presentation centered on the Ignite Fellowship and other roles that students can join within TFA, where they can directly tutor children around the country and make a real impact. Students also participated in a tutor and student scenario, acting out different learner types and figuring out how to effectively guide them through problems!

IrisHacks 2023
On May 6th and 7th, WICS’ Community Outreach hosted, in collaboration with PCWOC, a 2-day hackathon for beginner hackers. 13 UCI, community college, and high school students came to the UCI campus to work together to build a project from scratch. The participants attended a short workshop series introducing fundamental tools, such as HTML/CSS, Git, SQL, and APIs, that they could use in their projects. After 24 hours of hacking, the students presented their project to a panel of judges to win some prizes. Congrats to all the winners!

NetWICS 2023
This spring quarter, the WICS Community Outreach Committee hosted our first-ever NetWICS, a conference aimed at introducing computer science career paths to high school girls. We had over 15 participants join our conference, where they learned about various topics such as web development and UI/UX. We invited members of WICS to volunteer as mentors for the high school girls. We also partnered with ICS clubs like Design at UCI, Data at UCI, Commit the Change, and Cyber at UCI. Overall, Outreach hosted a successful first conference! Special shoutout to our committee members Kristen Yee and Teresa Hempen for leading and organizing this event.

Scratch-a-thon 2026
WICS Outreach members had the pleasure of hosting 6-8th grade students from El Sol Academy, teaching them about coding fundamentals through Scratch. On Day 1, Outreach members presented interactive lessons to familiarize students with the Scratch language. We also took a walk around campus to show them more of UCI. On Day 2, the students spent their time creating their own game or story with the Scratch skills they practiced the day before. Winners of various project categories were given a prize as well! The students greatly enjoyed delving into beginner-level coding, interacting with their peers, and experiencing mentorship from WICS members.
Highlights:
learning about coding basics such as variables and loops on Day 1
taking a tour around Ring Road and taking a picture with Peter the Anteater near Student Center
Kahoot review on Day 2 about Scratch
working on projects
doing a gallery walk within the students and playing each others’ games
Check out some photos in
our photo gallery
!
Outreach members helping students through an activity

About Community Outreach
WICS is committed to helping students learn about computing. To this end, Outreach Committee continues to create curriculum and gather resources geared towards encouraging underrepresented minorities to explore STEM. We host both day-long and multi-day workshops (Hour of Code, AspireIT) for middle school girls and lead various programs for K-12 and college students.
We have been awarded the following grants from
Google and the National Center for Women and Information (NCWIT)
to support and lead the respective outreach programs:
AspireIT ($3000 from NCWIT)
funds a series of app development workshops using AppInventor to introduce middle school students to computing. WICS collaborated with the Girls Who Code Club of Woodbridge High School.
Years awarded: 2015−2016, 2016−2017, 2017−2018
IgniteCS ($8790 from Google)
funds a third year of our high school program, where we host workshops to educate girls about ICS and career paths and to introduce them to programming and building progress
Years awarded: 2013−2014, 2014−2015, 2016−2017
Trailblazer Award ($15,000 from NCWIT & Google.org)
funds a new program, ExploreICS, for Undergraduate/Undeclared students to show them what ICS is, what careers are possible, and what are the different majors and minors in the Donald Bren School of ICS.
Years awarded:  2016−2017

April 20 @ 6:30 pm
-
8:00 pm
RSVP:
https://bit.ly/WICSxMECPSLabTourS26
Stamped Event?: Yes
Join WICS and MECPS for an interactive evening in Engineering Hall featuring a tour of the Center for Resilient Autonomous Systems lab, including demonstrations with a humanoid robot and robot dog. Attendees will also hear about Professor Burke’s ECPS 295 Drone course, enjoy pizza and refreshments, and rotate through activities in small groups. Please RSVP using your UCI email.

Fall 2025 Week 7: AI@UCI x WICS Spot the Bot
11/10/2025 @ 6:30 pm
-
8:00 pm
RSVP:
https://bit.ly/WICSxAI@UCIF25
Stamped Event?: Yes
Join AI@UCI and WICS for a fun and interactive evening exploring the world of AI! Learn about pioneering women in AI and discover how chatbots work before diving into our main game, AI Among Us, where you’ll try to spot which answer was written by an AI. Stick around for speed networking and a fast-paced Kahoot! trivia session with prizes. Don’t miss this chance to learn, connect, and play!

-
5:00 pm

Stamped Event?: No
Come destress before finals week at our Aldrich Mentorship Social! Whether you want to participate in small games and activities to connect with other mentorship families or reflect on your fruitful connections this quarter with your own family, we invite you to stop by! We will be doing structured activities in the first hour and socializing time afterwards, so make sure to coordinate with your fams beforehand so everyone can participate!
Some small snacks will be provided while supplies last, but we encourage you to bring your own food to share! Remember to bring your own blankets & chairs, and we’ll see you soon!

Skip to content
Katie Yeh
What did you do for WICS? (events, new ideas, etc)
I was part of WICS for the four years I was at UC Irvine. I first joined as a committee member as a freshman, my junior year I decided to run for External Vice President and then President for my senior year. It was great because I was part of the marketing committee my first two years so as External VP it was helpful to plan events with companies and then work with the marketing committee to get all the information out to our members.
Why do you think WICS is helpful?
WICS was a big part of my college experience and it was helpful in connecting me with other people who had the same interests. I was able to get valuable advice from existing members of WICS and they became some of my closest friends, who I still keep in touch with even after college. WICS holds many useful workshops and info sessions, such as networking workshops or resume workshops and sometimes they are partnered with companies so students can get direct tips and feedback from someone in the industry.
I think all of our events were a huge success and it’s so hard to just choose one, ranging from our weekly meetings to allowing 30 students to attend Grace Hopper to holding our own CWIC conference. I think they all were successful in their own way. A study session can seem so minuscule but to some it was a great way to find peers in the same class that they have never met before. Grace Hopper, I can’t even describe how wonderful that opportunity is and how fortunate WICS is to receive the grant to allow so many students to attend. Then, there’s CWIC, where my co-president, Ericca Go, and I decided to host the biannual conference at UC Irvine. We had, I believe, about 150 attendees and it was a great turnout. It was a way for us to bring a mini version of Grace Hopper to local schools to support and inspire everyone around.
What do you do now? What are some achievements you’d like to share?
Currently, I am a software engineer at Intuit working on TurboTax Live. While at Intuit, I’m glad to say that I am still part of helping the community by hosting monthly meetups for Women of San Diego JavaScript and joined the Intuit UCSD mentorship program, where I now mentor 3 female UCSD students.
Do you have any words of wisdom to share to females in STEM?
If you ever have any doubt if this is the right field for you, I want to tell you yes it is and you can do it. Don’t let other people get you down and don’t be afraid to reach out for mentors.
×

Skip to content
Katie Khu
Why do you think WICS is helpful?
I wanted to join WICS because I was originally a Pharmaceutical Sciences major who decided to switch to Computer Science, and I had no idea how I would navigate this whole new world. WICS provided me with a plethora of opportunities to see different career paths and converse with students and companies. The organization essentially supported me in my transition to software engineering, and I still keep in touch with the friends that I’ve made from the events, some of whom I am currently working in the same state with. Most of all, I received my internship and job opportunities as a result of being in WICS.
What did you do for WICS? (events, new ideas, etc)
I first started out as a WICS general member and excitedly joined the committee, where I was placed into the Corporate Committee. Then I ran for Treasurer before I started leading WICS as co-president in 2016-2017. I was always excited to work on outreach projects, because I knew first-hand how overwhelming computer science can seem, especially in the beginning. Therefore, I focused heavily on outreach and co-wrote a proposal that won the $10,000 Trailblazer Award from NCWIT, which were used to create a mentorship program for Undeclared majors here at UCI.
Were there any memorable events that you felt were a huge success? What did you like about them?
One of my favorite events was a social game night where we just brought in a bunch of board games and food and just enjoyed the time. A few others even surprised the group with Super Smash Bros and that really added to the community spirit that were prevalent that night. It was definitely a great way to get to know people!
What do you do now? What are some achievements youâ€™d like to share?
I am a Software Development Engineer II at Expedia Group, where I work on full-stack applications and know the difference between backend and frontend. ðŸ™‚ I’ve also developed some skills in ping pong and foosball!
Do you have any words of wisdom to share to females in STEM?
When I switched majors to Computer Science, I was in my third year and always felt like I was behind. Whenever I introduced myself as a junior, I had to include that I had just switched majors. Lots of imposter syndrome.
If you’re ever in that position, you have to push through that initial uncomfortable start, because it will get so much better. I remember my struggles to even understand basic CS concepts in the beginning, and now it feels so great to pick things up quickly as I become familiarized with different technologies. It also helps to have some support, whether that means coming to a WICS meeting for the first time, speaking to your classmate that sits on the right, or coming to your professor/TA’s office hours. It is immensely important to ask questions and let people know when you need help or don’t understand something – we all started from somewhere.
For those that are headed to their first job, I will give you similar advice. Be okay with not knowing much, but immerse yourself and you’ll look back a year later and see how much you have grown. As an entry-level engineer, you are not expected to know everything, but do try to put some effort in to look up things that are foreign to you. When it takes a lot more time to understand something, it would be much better to speak to someone more senior and have them clarify the concept/tech. Good luck and have fun in the process!
×

Skip to content
Janay Nunez
What did you do for WICS? (events, new ideas, etc)
Katie Khuu and I switched the Projects committee to the Community Outreach committee, and at the end of our tenure made the position require two people to head it. We had a board retreat over the summer to plan events, and when we interviewed people for committee, we opened it up to the rest of board members to interview them as well. We also applied for and got a $22k NCWIT grants (student seed fund I think it was?), as well as secured Google and Facebook as corporate sponsors for the first time.
Why do you think WICS is helpful?
I was scared to send emails when I first started, going from that to becoming president and having to send emails all the time was a huge journey for me. I also learned a lot about leadership and working with others, both from being led and then learning what I liked/disliked from that, and then applying it as Marketing chair and then President.
I also didn’t know I could be a president until I was. I was scared of running because of the public speaking aspect of being a president. But then the more I thought about it, the more I felt like I really had something to contribute, and that if I didn’t try, I would be doing myself and club a disservice.
Were there any memorable events that you felt were a huge success? What did you like about them?
I’m totally drawing a blank – everything? There were a lot of events we already carried on from previous years like WICS Games and Mock Tech Interviews that I really loved. Oh wait – I was super proud of our first WICS Retreat that we did in Lake Arrowhead (I think that was the place…). That felt like a huge accomplishment because we had always wanted one and never had one and the trip actually went well and people liked it!
What do you do now? What are some achievements you’d like to share?
I work as a software engineer at Google on the Play Store for Android TV. Our redesign just got announced at Google I/O here:
https://9to5google.com/2019/05/07/android-tv-play-store-redesign/
Do you have any words of wisdom to share to females in STEM?
Believe in yourself! I almost didn’t apply to Google because I was terrified of the interview process and didn’t think I could get in. I really mean that – I did not think I could make it AT ALL. But then I thought, what do you have to lose? And boom, I did it.
×

Spring 2026 Week 5: 2026 Grace Hopper Celebration Info Session
April 29 @ 2:00 pm
-
3:00 pm
RSVP:
https://bit.ly/WICSxGHCInfoS26
Stamped Event?: No
Interested in attending the world’s largest conference for women in tech?
Attend WICS’ info session on the Grace Hopper Celebration on Wednesday, April 29th from 2–3 PM in DBH 3011!
We will provide information on how to apply for the scholarship that funds your attendance at the conference. Additionally, you will hear from past GHC attendees about their experiences and tips for making the most of GHC.
We encourage all ICS students to apply for this opportunity to network and learn more about the tech industry!

Spring 2026 Week 5: WICS 30th Anniversary
April 27 @ 6:30 pm
-
8:30 pm
RSVP:
https://bit.ly/WICS30thAnniversaryS26
Stamped Event?: No
WICS turned 30 this year, and we want YOU to celebrate with us!
Join us for an evening honoring three decades of community, mentorship, and women in computing. Hear from WICS’ founder, Professor Debra J. Richardson, ICS Dean Marios, and other leaders who have shaped this organization since 1995, while enjoying light bites and good conversations.
Monday, April 27 | 6:30–8:30 PM | ISEB Auditorium
Be sure to RSVP! Hope to see you there
#WICS30Years #WomeninCS #UCI #ICS

Fall 2024: Scratch-a-thon Volunteer Applications
Application: bit.ly/WICSxScratchathonVolunteerApplicationF24 Hi everyone! WICS Outreach will be hosting a Scratch-a-thon on November 16th - 17th for 6-8th graders and we are looking for volunteers! Located at UCI in ICS2 152. Any experience level is welcome. Please use the URL in our LinkTree to sign up :)

April 13 @ 6:30 pm
-
8:00 pm
RSVP:
https://bit.ly/WICSxTeachForAmericaS26
Stamped Event?: Yes
Interested in making an impact through education? Join WICS and Teach For America to learn more about what tutoring and academic support can look like! We’ll get a chance to simulate tutoring sessions and learn how to support students, decorate encouraging end-of-the-year cards for local schools, and hear about opportunities for college students at Teach For America. We hope to see you all there!
Learn more about Teach For America here:
https://www.teachforamerica.org/

Skip to content
Women in Information and Computer Sciences
(
WICS
) is a social and professional non-profit
organization at UCI established to help and encourage women to pursue a college degree and
a successful career in the computer science field.
We provide support for one another and have various activities such as:
Professional Workshops
(eg. Interview, Resume, Networking Workshops)
Technical Workshops
(Web development, mobile, open source)
Mock Technical Interviews
(get experience and feedback so you can blow away interviewers when it really counts!)
Company Involvement
– Information Sessions, Tours, Luncheons, and Dinners (eg. Google, Blizzard Entertainment, Western Digital, and many others)
Job and Internship Resources
(we receive many internship and job offers exclusively for our members)
Numerous Networking Events and Opportunities
– as companies receive thousands upon thousands of resumes, they are turning increasingly to referrals and networking for their new hires
Community Outreach Programs
(AWT [Association of Women in Technology] Girl Scout Computer Workshops, various events with Girls Inc. of Orange County for elementary, middle, and high school students)
Conference Sponsorship
(we fundraise and find sponsors to send us to technical conferences!)
Educational Events
(research opportunities, graduate school, internships, other informational sessions)
Social Gatherings
(dinner and movie nights, karaoke, board game or video game nights, informal gatherings, study breaks, etc.)
Study groups, Tutoring, and Mentorship
Free Food!
(refreshments are almost always served at our events; however, delicious food and great networking opportunities are available through many of our events.)
We share laughs, have fun, eat, learn, and work together! Come out and get to know other students, faculty (including professors and counselors), company representatives, and alumni! Learn from the experiences of others and get ahead. Although we will emphasize our focus on women, everyone is welcome to join us!
1st/2nd year students
: We’re not that scary/intimidating — don’t worry. We have fun together and help each other out!
3rd/4th+ year students
: We’ll help you find your path and achieve your goals!
Grad students
: You are always welcome to our events, they can be beneficial to you and you may be beneficial to us.

History of WICS
Women in Information and Computer Sciences (WICS) was established in 1995. Dr. Debra Richardson, longtime advisor of WICS, was teaching a class of about 100 ICS students, and only 6 of them were women. Seeing that there was both a huge gender gap in the ICS school and a retention issue with females in ICS, Dr. Richardson and one of her graduate students decided to form WICS. Back then, meetings were merely a small group of girls getting together for lunch a few times a year as a means of support.
WICS has grown quite a bit since then. With a much bigger group involved and more succession planning, WICS has become a strong organization dedicated to encouraging women to pursue the computer science field and providing them with the support to achieve these goals. As of 2023, with 13 board members and 4 committees (external affairs, internal affairs, community outreach, and marketing), WICS is able to make a larger impact on female ICS students and support them while they build up their careers in the technological field.
WICS Alumni
Janay Nunez
Software Engineer, Google
I also learned a lot about leadership and working with others, both from being led and then learning what I liked/disliked from that.
Katie Khu
Software Engineer, Expedia Group
I wanted to join WICS because I was originally a Pharmaceutical Sciences major who decided to switch to Computer Science, and I had no idea how I would navigate this whole new world.
Katie Yeh
Software Engineer, Intuit
I was able to get valuable advice from existing members of WICS and they became some of my closest friends, who I still keep in touch with even after college.
